mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over a 6-month period signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate over a 3-month period signals a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) over a 6-month period signals rising supply and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in the number of housing starts (HOUST) over a 3-month period signals rising demand and economic growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) over a 3-month period signals a tightening monetary cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Real Disposable Personal Income (RSAFS) over a 6-month period, combined with a 1% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) over the same period, signals rising demand and inflation expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 2% and CPIAUCSL rises > 1% in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a 3-month period, combined with a 2% increase in the number of payroll employees (PAYEMS) over the same period, signals a tightening labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5% and PAYEMS rises > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
